records.title: 30-day readmission rate
records.summary: Monitor readmission rate by diagnosis and care pathway to identify preventable returns.
records.metric_definition: Share of patients readmitted within 30 days of discharge.

records.title: Claims denial rate
records.summary: Track denial rate and overturn success by payer and denial category.

records.title: Clinical traceability by default
records.summary: Any recommendation impacting patient outcomes should preserve lineage to source clinical events and model rationale.

records.title: Protect privacy in every analytical step
records.summary: Use minimum necessary data access and role-safe controls for PHI-bearing workflows.

records.title: Outcome-first care operations
records.summary: Prioritize interventions that simultaneously improve patient outcomes and throughput utilization.

records.title: Provider burden reduction
records.summary: Use automation to reduce documentation burden while preserving coding quality and auditability.

records.title: Favor use cases operable on de-identified data, reserving PHI for clinical decisions
records.summary: Prioritize use cases that deliver value on aggregated or de-identified data sets — population health trends, operational throughput, supply forecasting — and reserve PHI-bearing analytics only for direct clinical decision support, reducing compliance exposure per use case.

records.title: Produce standardized clinical outcome measures reusable across departments
records.summary: Prefer use cases that output standardized, shareable outcome metrics — readmission rates, length of stay, mortality indices — consumable by quality, operations, and finance teams rather than department-specific one-off analyses.
